topics.title: Installation
topics.description: Livewire is a Laravel package, so you will need to have a Laravel application up and running before you can install and use Livewire. If you need help setting up a new Laravel application, please see the official Laravel documentation.
keywords: #alpine.js not available on pages without livewire components
#components not updating or errors in browser console

topics.title: Quickstart
topics.description: Livewire allows you to build dynamic, reactive interfaces using only PHP—no JavaScript required. Instead of writing frontend code in JavaScript frameworks, you write simple PHP classes and Blade templates, and Livewire handles all the complex JavaScript behind the scenes.

topics.title: Upgrade Guide
topics.description: Livewire v4 introduces several improvements and optimizations while maintaining backward compatibility wherever possible. This guide will help you upgrade from Livewire v3 to v4.

topics.title: Actions
topics.description: Livewire actions are methods on your component that can be triggered by frontend interactions like clicking a button or submitting a form. They provide the developer experience of being able to call a PHP method directly from the browser, allowing you to focus on the logic of your application without getting bogged down writing boilerplate code connecting your application's frontend and backend.

topics.title: Components
topics.description: Livewire components are essentially PHP classes with properties and methods that can be called directly from a Blade template. This powerful combination allows you to create full-stack interactive interfaces with a fraction of the effort and complexity of modern JavaScript alternatives.

topics.title: Events
topics.description: Livewire offers a robust event system that you can use to communicate between different components on the page. Because it uses browser events under the hood, you can also use Livewire's event system to communicate with Alpine components or even plain, vanilla JavaScript.

topics.title: Forms
topics.description: Because forms are the backbone of most web applications, Livewire provides loads of helpful utilities for building them. From handling simple input elements to complex things like real-time validation or file uploading, Livewire has simple, well-documented tools to make your life easier and delight your users.

topics.title: Lifecycle Hooks
topics.description: Livewire provides a variety of lifecycle hooks that allow you to execute code at specific points during a component's lifecycle. These hooks enable you to perform actions before or after particular events, such as initializing the component, updating properties, or rendering the template.

topics.title: Nesting Components
topics.description: Livewire allows you to nest additional Livewire components inside of a parent component. This feature is immensely powerful, as it allows you to re-use and encapsulate behavior within Livewire components that are shared across your application.
keywords: #directly accessing the parent from the child

topics.title: Pages
topics.description: Livewire components can be used as entire pages by assigning them directly to routes. This allows you to build complete application pages using Livewire components, with additional capabilities like custom layouts, page titles, and route parameters.

topics.title: Properties
topics.description: Properties store and manage state inside your Livewire components. They are defined as public properties on component classes and can be accessed and modified on both the server and client-side.

topics.title: Session Properties
topics.description: Livewire makes it easy to persist property values across page refreshes/changes using the #[Session] attribute.

topics.title: Testing
topics.description: Livewire components are simple to test. Because they are just Laravel classes under the hood, they can be tested using Laravel's existing testing tools. However, Livewire provides many additional utilities to make testing your components a breeze.

topics.title: Nesting
topics.description: Like many other component-based frameworks, Livewire components are nestable — meaning one component can render multiple components within itself.

topics.title: Alpine
topics.description: AlpineJS is a lightweight JavaScript library that makes it easy to add client-side interactivity to your web pages. It was originally built to complement tools like Livewire where a more JavaScript-centric utility is helpful for sprinkling interactivity around your app.
keywords: #manually bundling alpine in your javascript build

topics.title: Async
topics.description: The #[Async] attribute allows actions to run in parallel without being queued, making them execute immediately even if other requests are in-flight.

topics.title: Computed
topics.description: The #[Computed] attribute allows you to create derived properties that are cached during a request, providing a performance advantage when accessing expensive operations multiple times.

topics.title: Defer
topics.description: The #[Defer] attribute makes a component load immediately after the initial page load is complete, preventing slow components from blocking the page render.

topics.title: Isolate
topics.description: The #[Isolate] attribute prevents a component's requests from being bundled with other component updates, allowing it to execute in parallel.

topics.title: Js
topics.description: The #[Js] attribute designates methods that return JavaScript code to be executed on the client-side. Methods marked with #[Js] can be called directly from your templates without making a server request.

topics.title: Json
topics.description: The #[Json] attribute marks an action as a JSON endpoint, returning data directly to JavaScript. Validation errors trigger a promise rejection with structured error data. This is ideal for actions consumed by JavaScript rather than rendered in Blade.

topics.title: Layout
topics.description: The #[Layout] attribute specifies which Blade layout a full-page component should use, allowing you to customize layouts on a per-component basis.

topics.title: Lazy
topics.description: The #[Lazy] attribute makes a component load only when it becomes visible in the viewport, preventing slow components from blocking the initial page render.

topics.title: Locked
topics.description: The #[Locked] attribute prevents properties from being modified on the client-side, protecting sensitive data like model IDs from tampering by users.

topics.title: Modelable
topics.description: The #[Modelable] attribute designates a property in a child component that can be bound to from a parent component using wire:model.

topics.title: On
topics.description: The #[On] attribute allows a component to listen for events and execute a method when those events are dispatched.

topics.title: Reactive
topics.description: The #[Reactive] attribute makes a child component's property automatically update when the parent changes the value being passed in.

topics.title: Renderless
topics.description: The #[Renderless] attribute skips the rendering phase of Livewire's lifecycle when an action is called, improving performance for actions that don't modify the component's view.

topics.title: Session
topics.description: The #[Session] attribute persists a property's value in the user's session, maintaining it across page refreshes and navigation.

topics.title: Title
topics.description: The #[Title] attribute sets the page title for full-page Livewire components.

topics.title: Transition
topics.description: The #[Transition] attribute configures view transition behavior for action methods, allowing you to set transition types or skip transitions entirely.

topics.title: Url
topics.description: The #[Url] attribute stores a property's value in the URL's query string, allowing users to share and bookmark specific states of a page.

topics.title: Validate
topics.description: The #[Validate] attribute associates validation rules with component properties, enabling automatic real-time validation and clean rule declaration.

topics.title: Request Bundling
topics.description: Every component update in Livewire triggers a network request. By default, when multiple components trigger updates at the same time, they are bundled into a single request.

topics.title: Computed Properties
topics.description: Computed properties are a way to create "derived" properties in Livewire. Like accessors on an Eloquent model, computed properties allow you to access values and memoize them for future access during the request.

topics.title: Contribution Guide
topics.description: Hi there and welcome to the Livewire contribution guide. In this guide, we are going to take a look at how you can contribute to Livewire by submitting new features, fixing failing tests, or resolving bugs.

topics.title: @island
topics.description: The @island directive creates isolated regions within a component that update independently, without re-rendering the entire component.

topics.title: @persist
topics.description: The @persist directive preserves elements across page navigations when using wire:navigate, maintaining their state and avoiding re-initialization.

topics.title: @placeholder
topics.description: The @placeholder directive displays custom content while lazy or deferred components and islands are loading.

topics.title: @teleport
topics.description: The @teleport directive renders a portion of your template in a different location in the DOM, outside the component's normal placement.

topics.title: File Downloads
topics.description: File downloads in Livewire work much the same as in Laravel itself. Typically, you can use any Laravel download utility inside a Livewire component, and it should work as expected.

topics.title: Islands
topics.description: Islands allow you to create isolated regions within a Livewire component that update independently. When an action occurs inside an island, only that island re-renders — not the entire component.

topics.title: Lazy Loading
topics.description: Livewire allows you to lazy load components that would otherwise slow down the initial page load.

topics.title: Loading States
topics.description: When a user interacts with your Livewire components, providing visual feedback during network requests is essential for a good user experience. Livewire automatically adds a data-loading attribute to any element that triggers a network request, making it easy to style loading states.

topics.title: Locked Properties
topics.description: Livewire properties are able to be modified freely on both the frontend and backend using utilities like wire:model. If you want to prevent a property — like a model ID — from being modified on the frontend, you can use Livewire's #[Locked] attribute.

topics.title: Navigate
topics.description: Many modern web applications are built as "single page applications" (SPAs). In these applications, each page rendered by the application no longer requires a full browser page reload, avoiding the overhead of re-downloading JavaScript and CSS assets on every request.

topics.title: Offline States
topics.description: In real-time applications, it can be helpful to provide a visual indication that the user's device is no longer connected to the internet.

topics.title: Package Development
topics.description: To include Livewire components in a Laravel package, you'll need to register them in your package's service provider.

topics.title: Pagination
topics.description: Laravel's pagination feature allows you to query a subset of data and provides your users with the ability to navigate between pages of those results.

topics.title: Redirecting
topics.description: After a user performs some action — like submitting a form — you may want to redirect them to another page in your application.

topics.title: Styles
topics.description: Livewire allows you to include component-specific styles directly in your single-file and multi-file components. These styles are automatically scoped to your component, preventing them from leaking into other parts of your application.

topics.title: Synthesizers
topics.description: Because Livewire components are dehydrated (serialized) into JSON, then hydrated (unserialized) back into PHP components between requests, their properties need to be JSON-serializable.

topics.title: Teleport
topics.description: Livewire allows you to teleport part of your template to another part of the DOM on the page entirely.

topics.title: File Uploads
topics.description: Livewire offers powerful support for uploading files within your components.

topics.title: URL Query Parameters
topics.description: Livewire allows you to store component properties in the URL's query string. For example, you may want a $search property in your component to be included in the URL: https://example.com/users?search=bob. This is particularly useful for things like filtering, sorting, and pagination, as it allows users to share and bookmark specific states of a page.

topics.title: Validation
topics.description: Livewire aims to make validating a user's input and giving them feedback as pleasant as possible. By building on top of Laravel's validation features, Livewire leverages your existing knowledge while also providing you with robust, additional features like real-time validation.

topics.title: wire:bind
topics.description: wire:bind is a directive that dynamically binds HTML attributes to component properties or expressions. Unlike using Blade's attribute syntax, wire:bind updates the attribute reactively on the client without requiring a full re-render.

topics.title: wire:click
topics.description: Livewire provides a simple wire:click directive for calling component methods (aka actions) when a user clicks a specific element on the page.

topics.title: wire:cloak
topics.description: wire:cloak is a directive that hides elements on page load until Livewire is fully initialized. This is useful for preventing the "flash of unstyled content" that can occur when the page loads before Livewire has a chance to initialize.

topics.title: wire:confirm
topics.description: Before performing dangerous actions in Livewire, you may want to provide your users with some sort of visual confirmation.

topics.title: wire:current
topics.description: The wire:current directive allows you to easily detect and style currently active links on a page.

topics.title: wire:dirty
topics.description: In a traditional HTML page containing a form, the form is only ever submitted when the user presses the "Submit" button.

topics.title: wire:ignore
topics.description: Livewire's ability to make updates to the page is what makes it "live", however, there are times when you might want to prevent Livewire from updating a portion of the page.

topics.title: wire:init
topics.description: Livewire offers a wire:init directive to run an action as soon as the component is rendered. This can be helpful in cases where you don't want to hold up the entire page load, but want to load some data immediately after the page load.

topics.title: wire:intersect
topics.description: Livewire's wire:intersect directive allows you to execute an action when an element enters or leaves the viewport. This is useful for lazy loading content, triggering analytics, or creating scroll-based interactions.

topics.title: wire:loading
topics.description: Loading indicators are an important part of crafting good user interfaces. They give users visual feedback when a request is being made to the server, so they know they are waiting for a process to complete.

topics.title: wire:model
topics.description: Livewire makes it easy to bind a component property's value with form inputs using wire:model.

topics.title: wire:navigate
topics.description: Livewire's wire:navigate feature makes page navigation much faster, providing an SPA-like experience for your users.

topics.title: wire:offline
topics.description: In real-time applications, it can be helpful to provide a visual indication that the user's device is no longer connected to the internet.

topics.title: wire:poll
topics.description: Polling is a technique used in web applications to "poll" the server (send requests on a regular interval) for updates. It's a simple way to keep a page up-to-date without the need for a more sophisticated technology like WebSockets.

topics.title: wire:ref
topics.description: Refs in Livewire provide a way to name, then target an individual element or component inside Livewire.

topics.title: wire:replace
topics.description: Livewire's DOM diffing is useful for updating existing elements on your page, but occasionally you may need to force some elements to render from scratch to reset internal state.

topics.title: wire:show
topics.description: Livewire's wire:show directive makes it easy to show and hide elements based on the result of an expression.

topics.title: wire:sort
topics.description: Livewire provides drag-and-drop sorting through the wire:sort directive. Add it to a parent element and use wire:sort:item on each child to make lists sortable with smooth animations out of the box.

topics.title: wire:stream
topics.description: Livewire allows you to stream content to a web page before a request is complete via the wire:stream API. This is an extremely useful feature for things like AI chat-bots which stream responses as they are generated.

topics.title: wire:submit
topics.description: Livewire makes it easy to handle form submissions via the wire:submit directive. By adding wire:submit to a <form> element, Livewire will intercept the form submission, prevent the default browser handling, and call any Livewire component method.

topics.title: wire:text
topics.description: wire:text is a directive that dynamically updates an element's text content based on a component property or expression. Unlike using Blade's {{ }} syntax, wire:text updates the content without requiring a network roundtrip to re-render the component.

topics.title: wire:transition
topics.description: wire:transition enables smooth animations when elements appear, disappear, or change using the browser's native View Transitions API.

topics.title: Volt
topics.description: Before using Volt, we recommend getting familiar with standard, class-based Livewire usage. This will allow you to quickly transfer your knowledge of Livewire into writing components using Volt's functional API.

topics.title: Contribution Guide
topics.description: Hi there and welcome to the Livewire contribution guide. In this guide, we are going to take a look at how you can contribute to Livewire by submitting new features, fixing failing tests, or resolving bugs.

topics.title: CSP
topics.description: Livewire offers a CSP-safe build that allows you to use Livewire applications in environments with strict Content Security Policy (CSP) headers that prohibit 'unsafe-eval'.

topics.title: Hydration
topics.description: Using Livewire feels like attaching a server-side PHP class directly to a web browser. Things like calling server-side functions directly from button presses support this illusion. But in reality, it is just that: an illusion.

topics.title: JavaScript
topics.description: Livewire and Alpine provide plenty of utilities for building dynamic components directly in your HTML, however, there are times when it's helpful to break out of the HTML and execute plain JavaScript for your component.

topics.title: Morphing
topics.description: When a Livewire component updates the browser's DOM, it does so in an intelligent way we call "morphing". The term morph is in contrast with a word like replace.

topics.title: Security
topics.description: It's important to make sure your Livewire apps are secure and don't expose any application vulnerabilities. Livewire has internal security features to handle many cases, however, there are times when it's up to your application code to keep your components secure.

topics.title: Synthesizers
topics.description: Because Livewire components are dehydrated (serialized) into JSON, then hydrated (unserialized) back into PHP components between requests, their properties need to be JSON-serializable.

topics.title: Troubleshooting
topics.description: Here at Livewire HQ, we try to remove problems from your pathway before you hit them. However, sometimes, there are some problems that we can't solve without introducing new ones, and other times, there are problems we can't anticipate.

directives.name: wire:bind
directives.description: Bind a property value to an element attribute

directives.name: wire:click
directives.description: Trigger a component action when the element is clicked

directives.name: wire:cloak
directives.description: Hide an element until Livewire has fully initialized on the page

directives.name: wire:confirm
directives.description: Show a confirmation dialog before executing an action

directives.name: wire:current
directives.description: Mark navigation elements as active based on the current URL

directives.name: wire:dirty
directives.description: Show, hide, or modify elements when form data has been changed

directives.name: wire:ignore
directives.description: Exclude an element from Livewire's DOM diffing/morphing

directives.name: wire:init
directives.description: Execute a component action when the component is first rendered

directives.name: wire:intersect
directives.description: Trigger an action when an element enters the browser viewport

directives.name: wire:key
directives.description: Provide a unique identifier for elements in loops to help Livewire track changes

directives.name: wire:loading
directives.description: Show, hide, or modify elements while a component is processing a request

directives.name: wire:model
directives.description: Bind an input element's value to a component property for two-way data binding

directives.name: wire:navigate
directives.description: Enable SPA-style navigation without full page reloads

directives.name: wire:offline
directives.description: Show, hide, or modify elements when the browser loses network connection

directives.name: wire:poll
directives.description: Automatically refresh component data at specified intervals

directives.name: wire:ref
directives.description: Create a named reference to an element for use in component PHP code

directives.name: wire:replace
directives.description: Replace the entire element instead of morphing on updates

directives.name: wire:show
directives.description: Toggle element visibility based on a component property value

directives.name: wire:sort
directives.description: Enable drag-and-drop sorting on a list of items

directives.name: wire:stream
directives.description: Stream content updates to a specific element for real-time UI updates

directives.name: wire:submit
directives.description: Handle form submission and trigger a component action

directives.name: wire:target
directives.description: Scope loading indicators to specific actions or properties

directives.name: wire:text
directives.description: Bind a component property as the text content of an element

directives.name: wire:transition
directives.description: Apply CSS transitions when elements are added or removed
